page_content:
PERS202 Intermediate Persian II (3 Credits) Further development of speaking, writing, listening and cultural knowledge, with special focus on culture. Prerequisite: PERS201; or must have appropriate World Language Placement (WLP). Restriction: Must not be native/fluent speaker of Persian.

PERS251 Modern Iran (3 Credits) General sociopolitical introduction to modern Iran from establishment of the Qajar dynasty in the late 18th century to the present day. Taught in English. Cross-listed with: HIST219X. Credit Only Granted for: PERS251 or HIST219X.

PERS252 Gender and Body in Iran (3 Credits) Multidisciplinary approach to key topics concerning gender and body in Iran, to include insights from religion, history, sociology, anthropology, disability studies, travel literature, arts, and fashion. Taught in English. Credit Only Granted for: WMST298J, or PERS252.

PERS269 Special Topics in Study Abroad II (1-6 Credits) Special topics course taken as part of an approved study abroad program. Repeatable to: 15 credits if content differs.
